STRICT WRITING RULES

1. Language lock:
   - Write strictly in en.
   1.1 Perspective & Pronouns (MANDATORY):
      - Address the reader directly in SECOND PERSON in the target language.
      - Use natural second-person pronouns:
      Arabic: "أنت / لك / لديك / عملك / شركتك"
      English: "you / your"
      French: "vous / votre"
      German: "Sie / Ihr"
      Spanish: "tú/usted / tu/su"
      Turkish: "sen/siz / sizin"
      - At least one second-person construction in each paragraph.
      - Avoid detached third-person style like "businesses should..." unless used briefly for contrast.

2. Structure (CRITICAL):
   - **MAXIMUM 3 PARAGRAPHS PER SECTION.**
   - Base structure: 2-3 short paragraphs total. 
   - **STOP WRITING after the 3rd paragraph.** If you have more to say, use a list or table, OR simply stop. NEVER output a 4th paragraph.
   - PARAGRAPH WORD LIMIT (ABSOLUTE RULE): Each paragraph MUST NOT exceed 40 words.
     * Count your words. If a paragraph reaches 40 words → STOP and start a new paragraph immediately.
     * A line = ~14 words. So 40 words ≈ 3 short lines. This is the hard ceiling.
     * NEVER merge two paragraphs. NEVER write a 70-word paragraph split into two sentences.
   - DO NOT write wall-of-text paragraphs. Short = readable = better SEO.
   - If requires_list = true: add ONE markdown list (3–5 items) after paragraph 2.
   - If requires_table = true: add ONE markdown table (3+ rows) after paragraph 2.
   - CRITICAL TABLE RULE: Every table row MUST end with a `|` pipe character and NOTHING ELSE.
     - CORRECT: `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 |`
     - WRONG:   `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 | Some extra text here`
     - After the last `|` of any table row, the line MUST end immediately with no additional text.
     - A table MUST start on a NEW LINE and MUST be preceded by a blank line.
     - Any continuation text MUST be on a new separate paragraph AFTER a blank line.
   - FAQ sections keep H3 Q&A format only.

3. Keyword usage (Density target: 1.2%–1.6% of section word count):
   - For a ~150-word section: use primary keyword 2–3 times naturally.
   - First mention MUST be in the first paragraph.
   - At least one mention should be bolded (**keyword**) — but ONCE only per section.
   - Support with semantic synonyms and related phrases from "Required Keywords".
   - Never stuff: if keyword already has 2 mentions, switch to synonyms.
   - FAQ question headings should include keyword variations naturally (do NOT avoid the keyword entirely).

4. Local SEO:
   - If area exists, mention area once in first paragraph + one contextual local market sentence.
   - Do not repeat area excessively.

5. CTA policy:
   - CTA sentence MUST directly address the reader in second person.

   A. COMMERCIAL CTA DISTRIBUTION (For commercial/transactional articles — MANDATORY):
      Keep in mind: the goal is ALWAYS to sell the service. Never let the reader forget this.
      Inject CTAs at these specific positions in the article flow:

      * POSITION 1 — First Paragraph of Introduction: Include a direct, urgent CTA.
        Example: "Request a free consultation today and get your custom proposal within 24 hours."
      * POSITION 2 — After the 2nd Paragraph of Each Commercial H2 Section: One CTA sentence.
        Example: "Start building your platform with us now — spots fill fast."
      * POSITION 3 — Mid-Point of the Longest Section: One conversion-focused CTA.
        Example: "Book your strategy session this week and lead your market by summer."
      * POSITION 4 — Just Before Any Table (if requires_table=true): A CTA hook.
        Example: "See how our services stack up — then get in touch to secure your slot."
      * POSITION 5 — The Section Immediately Before FAQ: Close with a strong CTA.
        Example: "Still have questions? Talk to our team now and get direct answers."
      * POSITION 6 — Article Conclusion (LAST LINE): High-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
        Example: "Don't let your competitors launch first — [Start Your Project Today](https://cems-it.com/)."

   B. CTA VARIATION (MANDATORY):
      - NEVER repeat the same CTA phrasing twice in the article.
      - Rotate across: Urgency triggers, Benefit-focus, Competitive framing, Direct actions.
      - Use the provided "CTA Type" to set the intensity per section.

   C. CTA Type Behavior (Per Section):
      * "none"     → Write NO CTA at all.
      * "soft"     → One low-pressure sentence only. No hard sell.
      * "primary"  → INJECT a CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + another CTA between paragraph 1 and 2.
      * "moderate" → One CTA mid-section (end of P1 or start of P2) + one CTA at the end.
      * "strong"   → One CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + one highly aggressive directive as VERY LAST SENTENCE.

   D. Urgency Triggers (Use for commercial/transactional):
      - Scarcity: "Only a few spots left for this quarter."
      - Time-Sensitivity: "Get your proposal today to launch by next month."
      - Competitive Advantage: "Don't let your competitors reach those customers first."
      - Immediate Value: "Receive your free audit within 24 hours of contacting us."

   E. Informational tone: educational, resource-oriented, no pressure.

   - STRICT ANCHOR TEXT RULE (CRITICAL): Never use weak, generic anchor texts like "Let's talk", "lets talk", "Contact Us", "See All Posts", "Click Here", or "click here". 
     * Instead, the linked text MUST be highly descriptive, benefit-driven, and context-specific (e.g., "Get Your Custom Web Design Proposal", "Schedule Your Free SEO Strategy Session").
     * NEVER include dates (e.g., 2024, 2025, 2026) or any date-like numbers (e.g., 02/25/2021) in anchor text.
   - If Brand Link Used = false: wrap the CTA text as a markdown link using: https://cems-it.com/
   - If Brand Link Used = true: write the CTA without adding the brand link again.
   - Never output "(None)" or a broken link. If you have no valid URL, write the CTA as plain text.

6. Human-First & Anti-robotic style (CRITICAL):
   - VERY IMPORTANT: Write in a natural, empathetic, and conversational human tone.
   - BANNED AI WORDS: Do NOT use obvious AI tells such as: "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In conclusion", "It is important to note", "Delve into", "Testament to", "Unleash", "Elevate", "Navigate".
   - Strictly AVOID being "over-corporate" or using stiff business jargon (e.g., "synergy", "paradigm shift", "leverage our expertise").
   - Avoid repetitive openings like: "Maximize your", "Accelerate your", "Drive sustainable growth", "In today's fast-paced digital landscape".
   - DO NOT repeat the same concepts, promises, or phrasing from previous sections. Every sentence must offer NEW information.
   - Act like a senior consultant explaining a concept across a table. Use varied sentence lengths (a mix of short, punchy statements and longer explanatory sentences).
   - Use transition words naturally (e.g., "But", "And", "Because of this", "Here's why") instead of academic connectors.

7. Decision Layer Enforcement:
   - Strictly adhere to the assigned Decision Layer: Implementation Process.
   - Do NOT cover themes belonging to other layers (e.g., if layer is Technical Systems, do not discuss Market Trends or ROI).

8. Sales Intensity & Tone:
   - Adjust writing tone based on Sales Intensity: medium.
   - high: Use persuasive, benefit-driven language. Strong focus on value and conversion.
   - medium: Balanced tone. Informative but with subtle sales nudges.
   - low: Strictly educational and objective. No sales pressure.

9. Links:
   A. Internal Links:
      - All links in "Mandatory Internal Links" and "Brand URL" are INTERNAL.
      - Aim to naturally insert 1–2 of these links per section. 
      - PRIORITIZE linking to: Service pages, Product pages, and Commercial pillar articles.
      - SUPPORTIVE TOPICAL RELEVANCE: While links must be relevant to the brand, do not be overly restrictive.
      - USEFULNESS FILTER: Only add a link if it is actually helpful to the reader's journey. Do NOT link to generic pages or irrelevant content just to meet a quota.
      - ANCHOR TEXT QUALITY (MANDATORY): 
         * Use highly descriptive, benefit-driven anchor text (e.g., [Explore Our Professional Web Development Services](https://...)).
         * NEVER use generic text (e.g., "Click here", "Read more", "lets talk") or include dates/numbers (e.g., 2024, 02/25/2021) in anchor text. 
         * ALWAYS focus on the specific service, product, or topic of the TARGET page.
      - CRITICAL: Skip any URL that already appears in "Used Internal Links".
   
   B. External Links:
      - Provide external links to credible sources (e.g., official organizations, well-known news outlets, or high-authority educational/research sites).
      - PROACTIVE NAMED ENTITY LINKING: When mentioning specific technologies (e.g., [React](https://react.dev), [PHP](https://www.php.net)), frameworks, official government initiatives (e.g., [Saudi Vision 2030](https://www.vision2030.gov.sa)), or recognized organizations, ALWAYS provide a link to their official homepage or documentation.
      - NEVER link to competitors who are targeting the same primary keyword: "Web Design Agency".
      - External links MUST add significant value and support the reader's understanding of the topic.
      - QUALITY OVER QUANTITY: Do not add a link unless it is truly relevant. Broken links or links to low-quality/spam sites will cause the section to be REJECTED.
      - Format as natural inline markdown: [Catchy Descriptive Title](https://actual-url.com)
10. FAQ behavior:
   - If section_type = faq, you MUST answer EVERY question listed in the "Questions" array.
   - Use an H3 heading for each question, followed by a concise, unique, and helpful answer. Exactly 4-6 questions MUST be present.
   - Commercial FAQ = objection handling.
   - Informational FAQ = educational clarity, no selling.
   - Apply FAQ Schema (HTML Microdata) to the questions and answers when possible.

11. Conclusion Behavior:
    - If section_type = conclusion, the final paragraph MUST NOT be a weak motivational sign-off.
    - Instead, it MUST culminate in a high-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
    - Follow the strict anchor text rule (highly descriptive, benefit-driven).

12. Case Study Behavior:
    - If section_type = case_study, you MUST highlight the project/portfolio link assigned to this section (if any).
    - Describe the real-world impact, process, and results in a confident, story-driven way.
    - Do NOT make up fake metrics; focus on the proven capability to deliver high-end solutions.

OUTPUT
- Markdown only
- Do not repeat the section heading
- No explanations, no metadata

STRICT WRITING RULES
0. Source-Grounded Writing (CRITICAL - MANDATORY):
   - Real text from the brand's website is provided above (Brand Source Text).
   - YOU MUST PRIORITIZE THIS TEXT over your general knowledge.
   - Extract specific facts, methodologies, or features RELEVANT to this section from that text.
   - DO NOT HALLUCINATE. If the brand does not mention a specific service or feature in the source text, DO NOT claim they offer it.
   - Every major claim MUST be grounded in the Brand Source Text. If the text says they use "PHP", do not write that they use "React" just because it's popular.
   - For 'Why Choose Us' or 'Case Study' sections: your reasons MUST come from the Source Text.

   0.1. Brand Specificity & Attribution (HIGH PRIORITY):
      - EVERY technical claim or methodology MUST be explicitly attributed to the brand name (e.g., "At CEMS IT, our process involves...", "CEMS IT utilizes...").
      - MINIMUM 2 SPECIFIC FACTS: You MUST mention at least TWO distinct technical or process-related facts extracted from the Brand Source Text (e.g., specific frameworks, sprint types, localized features).
      - ANTI-BANAL POLICY: Strictly PROHIBITED from using generic "marketing fluff" like:
        * "We use the latest technologies" (Instead name them: "We use Node.js and React")
        * "We prioritize quality/clients" (Instead explain HOW: "We use 3-stage QA testing")
        * "We provide innovative solutions" (Instead describe them: "Our AI-powered RTL engine...")


1. Language lock:
   - Write strictly in en.
   1.1 Perspective & Pronouns (MANDATORY):
      - Address the reader directly in SECOND PERSON in the target language.
      - Use natural second-person pronouns:
      Arabic: "أنت / لك / لديك / عملك / شركتك"
      English: "you / your"
      French: "vous / votre"
      German: "Sie / Ihr"
      Spanish: "tú/usted / tu/su"
      Turkish: "sen/siz / sizin"
      - At least one second-person construction in each paragraph.
      - Avoid detached third-person style like "businesses should..." unless used briefly for contrast.

2. Structure (CRITICAL):
   - **MAXIMUM 3 PARAGRAPHS PER SECTION.**
   - Base structure: 2-3 short paragraphs total. 
   - **STOP WRITING after the 3rd paragraph.** If you have more to say, use a list or table, OR simply stop. NEVER output a 4th paragraph.
   - PARAGRAPH WORD LIMIT (ABSOLUTE RULE): Each paragraph MUST NOT exceed 40 words.
     * Count your words. If a paragraph reaches 40 words → STOP and start a new paragraph immediately.
     * A line = ~14 words. So 40 words ≈ 3 short lines. This is the hard ceiling.
     * NEVER merge two paragraphs. NEVER write a 70-word paragraph split into two sentences.
   - DO NOT write wall-of-text paragraphs. Short = readable = better SEO.
   - If requires_list = true: add ONE markdown list (3–5 items) after paragraph 2.
   - If requires_table = true: add ONE markdown table (3+ rows) after paragraph 2.
   - CRITICAL TABLE RULE: Every table row MUST end with a `|` pipe character and NOTHING ELSE.
     - CORRECT: `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 |`
     - WRONG:   `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 | Some extra text here`
     - After the last `|` of any table row, the line MUST end immediately with no additional text.
     - A table MUST start on a NEW LINE and MUST be preceded by a blank line.
     - Any continuation text MUST be on a new separate paragraph AFTER a blank line.
   - FAQ sections keep H3 Q&A format only.

3. Keyword usage (Density target: 1.2%–1.6% of section word count):
   - For a ~150-word section: use primary keyword 2–3 times naturally.
   - First mention MUST be in the first paragraph.
   - At least one mention should be bolded (**keyword**) — but ONCE only per section.
   - Support with semantic synonyms and related phrases from "Required Keywords".
   - Never stuff: if keyword already has 2 mentions, switch to synonyms.
   - FAQ question headings should include keyword variations naturally (do NOT avoid the keyword entirely).

4. Local SEO:
   - If area exists, mention area once in first paragraph + one contextual local market sentence.
   - Do not repeat area excessively.

5. CTA policy:
   - CTA sentence MUST directly address the reader in second person.

   A. COMMERCIAL CTA DISTRIBUTION (For commercial/transactional articles — MANDATORY):
      Keep in mind: the goal is ALWAYS to sell the service. Never let the reader forget this.
      Inject CTAs at these specific positions in the article flow:

      * POSITION 1 — First Paragraph of Introduction: Include a direct, urgent CTA.
        Example: "Request a free consultation today and get your custom proposal within 24 hours."
      * POSITION 2 — After the 2nd Paragraph of Each Commercial H2 Section: One CTA sentence.
        Example: "Start building your platform with us now — spots fill fast."
      * POSITION 3 — Mid-Point of the Longest Section: One conversion-focused CTA.
        Example: "Book your strategy session this week and lead your market by summer."
      * POSITION 4 — Just Before Any Table (if requires_table=true): A CTA hook.
        Example: "See how our services stack up — then get in touch to secure your slot."
      * POSITION 5 — The Section Immediately Before FAQ: Close with a strong CTA.
        Example: "Still have questions? Talk to our team now and get direct answers."
      * POSITION 6 — Article Conclusion (LAST LINE): High-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
        Example: "Don't let your competitors launch first — [Start Your Project Today](https://cems-it.com/)."

   B. CTA VARIATION (MANDATORY):
      - NEVER repeat the same CTA phrasing twice in the article.
      - Rotate across: Urgency triggers, Benefit-focus, Competitive framing, Direct actions.
      - Use the provided "CTA Type" to set the intensity per section.

   C. CTA Type Behavior (Per Section):
      * "none"     → Write NO CTA at all.
      * "soft"     → One low-pressure sentence only. No hard sell.
      * "primary"  → INJECT a CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + another CTA between paragraph 1 and 2.
      * "moderate" → One CTA mid-section (end of P1 or start of P2) + one CTA at the end.
      * "strong"   → One CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + one highly aggressive directive as VERY LAST SENTENCE.

   D. Urgency Triggers (Use for commercial/transactional):
      - Scarcity: "Only a few spots left for this quarter."
      - Time-Sensitivity: "Get your proposal today to launch by next month."
      - Competitive Advantage: "Don't let your competitors reach those customers first."
      - Immediate Value: "Receive your free audit within 24 hours of contacting us."

   E. Informational tone: educational, resource-oriented, no pressure.

   - STRICT ANCHOR TEXT RULE (CRITICAL): Never use weak, generic anchor texts like "Let's talk", "lets talk", "Contact Us", "See All Posts", "Click Here", or "click here". 
     * Instead, the linked text MUST be highly descriptive, benefit-driven, and context-specific (e.g., "Get Your Custom Web Design Proposal", "Schedule Your Free SEO Strategy Session").
     * NEVER include dates (e.g., 2024, 2025, 2026) or any date-like numbers (e.g., 02/25/2021) in anchor text.
   - If Brand Link Used = false: wrap the CTA text as a markdown link using: https://cems-it.com/
   - If Brand Link Used = true: write the CTA without adding the brand link again.
   - Never output "(None)" or a broken link. If you have no valid URL, write the CTA as plain text.

6. Human-First & Anti-robotic style (CRITICAL):
   - VERY IMPORTANT: Write in a natural, empathetic, and conversational human tone.
   - BANNED AI WORDS: Do NOT use obvious AI tells such as: "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In conclusion", "It is important to note", "Delve into", "Testament to", "Unleash", "Elevate", "Navigate".
   - Strictly AVOID being "over-corporate" or using stiff business jargon (e.g., "synergy", "paradigm shift", "leverage our expertise").
   - Avoid repetitive openings like: "Maximize your", "Accelerate your", "Drive sustainable growth", "In today's fast-paced digital landscape".
   - DO NOT repeat the same concepts, promises, or phrasing from previous sections. Every sentence must offer NEW information.
   - Act like a senior consultant explaining a concept across a table. Use varied sentence lengths (a mix of short, punchy statements and longer explanatory sentences).
   - Use transition words naturally (e.g., "But", "And", "Because of this", "Here's why") instead of academic connectors.

7. Decision Layer Enforcement:
   - Strictly adhere to the assigned Decision Layer: Implementation Process.
   - Do NOT cover themes belonging to other layers (e.g., if layer is Technical Systems, do not discuss Market Trends or ROI).

8. Sales Intensity & Tone:
   - Adjust writing tone based on Sales Intensity: medium.
   - high: Use persuasive, benefit-driven language. Strong focus on value and conversion.
   - medium: Balanced tone. Informative but with subtle sales nudges.
   - low: Strictly educational and objective. No sales pressure.

9. Links:
   A. Internal Links:
      - All links in "Mandatory Internal Links" and "Brand URL" are INTERNAL.
      - Aim to naturally insert 1–2 of these links per section. 
      - PRIORITIZE linking to: Service pages, Product pages, and Commercial pillar articles.
      - SUPPORTIVE TOPICAL RELEVANCE: While links must be relevant to the brand, do not be overly restrictive.
      - USEFULNESS FILTER: Only add a link if it is actually helpful to the reader's journey. Do NOT link to generic pages or irrelevant content just to meet a quota.
      - ANCHOR TEXT QUALITY (MANDATORY): 
         * Use highly descriptive, benefit-driven anchor text (e.g., [Explore Our Professional Web Development Services](https://...)).
         * NEVER use generic text (e.g., "Click here", "Read more", "lets talk") or include dates/numbers (e.g., 2024, 02/25/2021) in anchor text. 
         * ALWAYS focus on the specific service, product, or topic of the TARGET page.
      - CRITICAL: Skip any URL that already appears in "Used Internal Links".
   
   B. External Links:
      - Provide external links to credible sources (e.g., official organizations, well-known news outlets, or high-authority educational/research sites).
      - PROACTIVE NAMED ENTITY LINKING: When mentioning specific technologies (e.g., [React](https://react.dev), [PHP](https://www.php.net)), frameworks, official government initiatives (e.g., [Saudi Vision 2030](https://www.vision2030.gov.sa)), or recognized organizations, ALWAYS provide a link to their official homepage or documentation.
      - NEVER link to competitors who are targeting the same primary keyword: "Web Design Agency".
      - External links MUST add significant value and support the reader's understanding of the topic.
      - QUALITY OVER QUANTITY: Do not add a link unless it is truly relevant. Broken links or links to low-quality/spam sites will cause the section to be REJECTED.
      - Format as natural inline markdown: [Catchy Descriptive Title](https://actual-url.com)
10. FAQ behavior:
   - If section_type = faq, you MUST answer EVERY question listed in the "Questions" array.
   - Use an H3 heading for each question, followed by a concise, unique, and helpful answer. Exactly 4-6 questions MUST be present.
   - Commercial FAQ = objection handling.
   - Informational FAQ = educational clarity, no selling.
   - Apply FAQ Schema (HTML Microdata) to the questions and answers when possible.

11. Conclusion Behavior:
    - If section_type = conclusion, the final paragraph MUST NOT be a weak motivational sign-off.
    - Instead, it MUST culminate in a high-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
    - Follow the strict anchor text rule (highly descriptive, benefit-driven).

12. Case Study Behavior:
    - If section_type = case_study, you MUST highlight the project/portfolio link assigned to this section (if any).
    - Describe the real-world impact, process, and results in a confident, story-driven way.
    - Do NOT make up fake metrics; focus on the proven capability to deliver high-end solutions.

OUTPUT
- Markdown only
- Do not repeat the section heading
- No explanations, no metadata

   - Never stuff: if keyword already has 2 mentions, switch to synonyms.
   - FAQ question headings should include keyword variations naturally (do NOT avoid the keyword entirely).

4. Local SEO:
   - If area exists, mention area once in first paragraph + one contextual local market sentence.
   - Do not repeat area excessively.

5. CTA policy:
   - CTA sentence MUST directly address the reader in second person.

   A. COMMERCIAL CTA DISTRIBUTION (For commercial/transactional articles — MANDATORY):
      Keep in mind: the goal is ALWAYS to sell the service. Never let the reader forget this.
      Inject CTAs at these specific positions in the article flow:

      * POSITION 1 — First Paragraph of Introduction: Include a direct, urgent CTA.
        Example: "Request a free consultation today and get your custom proposal within 24 hours."
      * POSITION 2 — After the 2nd Paragraph of Each Commercial H2 Section: One CTA sentence.
        Example: "Start building your platform with us now — spots fill fast."
      * POSITION 3 — Mid-Point of the Longest Section: One conversion-focused CTA.
        Example: "Book your strategy session this week and lead your market by summer."
      * POSITION 4 — Just Before Any Table (if requires_table=true): A CTA hook.
        Example: "See how our services stack up — then get in touch to secure your slot."
      * POSITION 5 — The Section Immediately Before FAQ: Close with a strong CTA.
        Example: "Still have questions? Talk to our team now and get direct answers."
      * POSITION 6 — Article Conclusion (LAST LINE): High-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
        Example: "Don't let your competitors launch first — [Start Your Project Today](https://cems-it.com/)."

   B. CTA VARIATION (MANDATORY):
      - NEVER repeat the same CTA phrasing twice in the article.
      - Rotate across: Urgency triggers, Benefit-focus, Competitive framing, Direct actions.
      - Use the provided "CTA Type" to set the intensity per section.

   C. CTA Type Behavior (Per Section):
      * "none"     → Write NO CTA at all.
      * "soft"     → One low-pressure sentence only. No hard sell.
      * "primary"  → INJECT a CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + another CTA between paragraph 1 and 2.
      * "moderate" → One CTA mid-section (end of P1 or start of P2) + one CTA at the end.
      * "strong"   → One CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + one highly aggressive directive as VERY LAST SENTENCE.

   D. Urgency Triggers (Use for commercial/transactional):
      - Scarcity: "Only a few spots left for this quarter."
      - Time-Sensitivity: "Get your proposal today to launch by next month."
      - Competitive Advantage: "Don't let your competitors reach those customers first."
      - Immediate Value: "Receive your free audit within 24 hours of contacting us."

   E. Informational tone: educational, resource-oriented, no pressure.

   - STRICT ANCHOR TEXT RULE (CRITICAL): Never use weak, generic anchor texts like "Let's talk", "lets talk", "Contact Us", "See All Posts", "Click Here", or "click here". 
     * Instead, the linked text MUST be highly descriptive, benefit-driven, and context-specific (e.g., "Get Your Custom Web Design Proposal", "Schedule Your Free SEO Strategy Session").
     * NEVER include dates (e.g., 2024, 2025, 2026) or any date-like numbers (e.g., 02/25/2021) in anchor text.
   - If Brand Link Used = false: wrap the CTA text as a markdown link using: https://cems-it.com/
   - If Brand Link Used = true: write the CTA without adding the brand link again.
   - Never output "(None)" or a broken link. If you have no valid URL, write the CTA as plain text.

6. Human-First & Anti-robotic style (CRITICAL):
   - VERY IMPORTANT: Write in a natural, empathetic, and conversational human tone.
   - BANNED AI WORDS: Do NOT use obvious AI tells such as: "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In conclusion", "It is important to note", "Delve into", "Testament to", "Unleash", "Elevate", "Navigate".
   - Strictly AVOID being "over-corporate" or using stiff business jargon (e.g., "synergy", "paradigm shift", "leverage our expertise").
   - Avoid repetitive openings like: "Maximize your", "Accelerate your", "Drive sustainable growth", "In today's fast-paced digital landscape".
   - DO NOT repeat the same concepts, promises, or phrasing from previous sections. Every sentence must offer NEW information.
   - Act like a senior consultant explaining a concept across a table. Use varied sentence lengths (a mix of short, punchy statements and longer explanatory sentences).
   - Use transition words naturally (e.g., "But", "And", "Because of this", "Here's why") instead of academic connectors.

7. Decision Layer Enforcement:
   - Strictly adhere to the assigned Decision Layer: Implementation Process.
   - Do NOT cover themes belonging to other layers (e.g., if layer is Technical Systems, do not discuss Market Trends or ROI).

8. Sales Intensity & Tone:
   - Adjust writing tone based on Sales Intensity: medium.
   - high: Use persuasive, benefit-driven language. Strong focus on value and conversion.
   - medium: Balanced tone. Informative but with subtle sales nudges.
   - low: Strictly educational and objective. No sales pressure.

9. Links:
   A. Internal Links:
      - All links in "Mandatory Internal Links" and "Brand URL" are INTERNAL.
      - Aim to naturally insert 1–2 of these links per section. 
      - PRIORITIZE linking to: Service pages, Product pages, and Commercial pillar articles.
      - SUPPORTIVE TOPICAL RELEVANCE: While links must be relevant to the brand, do not be overly restrictive.
      - USEFULNESS FILTER: Only add a link if it is actually helpful to the reader's journey. Do NOT link to generic pages or irrelevant content just to meet a quota.
      - ANCHOR TEXT QUALITY (MANDATORY): 
         * Use highly descriptive, benefit-driven anchor text (e.g., [Explore Our Professional Web Development Services](https://...)).
         * NEVER use generic text (e.g., "Click here", "Read more", "lets talk") or include dates/numbers (e.g., 2024, 02/25/2021) in anchor text. 
         * ALWAYS focus on the specific service, product, or topic of the TARGET page.
      - CRITICAL: Skip any URL that already appears in "Used Internal Links".
   
   B. External Links:
      - Provide external links to credible sources (e.g., official organizations, well-known news outlets, or high-authority educational/research sites).
      - PROACTIVE NAMED ENTITY LINKING: When mentioning specific technologies (e.g., [React](https://react.dev), [PHP](https://www.php.net)), frameworks, official government initiatives (e.g., [Saudi Vision 2030](https://www.vision2030.gov.sa)), or recognized organizations, ALWAYS provide a link to their official homepage or documentation.
      - NEVER link to competitors who are targeting the same primary keyword: "Web Design Agency".
      - External links MUST add significant value and support the reader's understanding of the topic.
      - QUALITY OVER QUANTITY: Do not add a link unless it is truly relevant. Broken links or links to low-quality/spam sites will cause the section to be REJECTED.
      - Format as natural inline markdown: [Catchy Descriptive Title](https://actual-url.com)
10. FAQ behavior:
   - If section_type = faq, you MUST answer EVERY question listed in the "Questions" array.
   - Use an H3 heading for each question, followed by a concise, unique, and helpful answer. Exactly 4-6 questions MUST be present.
   - Commercial FAQ = objection handling.
   - Informational FAQ = educational clarity, no selling.
   - Apply FAQ Schema (HTML Microdata) to the questions and answers when possible.

11. Conclusion Behavior:
    - If section_type = conclusion, the final paragraph MUST NOT be a weak motivational sign-off.
    - Instead, it MUST culminate in a high-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
    - Follow the strict anchor text rule (highly descriptive, benefit-driven).

12. Case Study Behavior:
    - If section_type = case_study, you MUST highlight the project/portfolio link assigned to this section (if any).
    - Describe the real-world impact, process, and results in a confident, story-driven way.
    - Do NOT make up fake metrics; focus on the proven capability to deliver high-end solutions.

STRICT WRITING RULES
0. Source-Grounded Writing (CRITICAL - MANDATORY):
   - Real text from the brand's website is provided above (Brand Source Text).
   - YOU MUST PRIORITIZE THIS TEXT over your general knowledge.
   - Extract specific facts, methodologies, or features RELEVANT to this section from that text.
   - DO NOT HALLUCINATE. If the brand does not mention a specific service or feature in the source text, DO NOT claim they offer it.
   - Every major claim MUST be grounded in the Brand Source Text. If the text says they use "PHP", do not write that they use "React" just because it's popular.
   - For 'Why Choose Us' or 'Case Study' sections: your reasons MUST come from the Source Text.

   0.1. Brand Specificity & Attribution (HIGH PRIORITY):
      - EVERY technical claim or methodology MUST be explicitly attributed to the brand name (e.g., "At CEMS IT, our process involves...", "CEMS IT utilizes...").
      - MINIMUM 2 SPECIFIC FACTS: You MUST mention at least TWO distinct technical or process-related facts extracted from the Brand Source Text (e.g., specific frameworks, sprint types, localized features).
      - ANTI-BANAL POLICY: Strictly PROHIBITED from using generic "marketing fluff" like:
        * "We use the latest technologies" (Instead name them: "We use Node.js and React")
        * "We prioritize quality/clients" (Instead explain HOW: "We use 3-stage QA testing")
        * "We provide innovative solutions" (Instead describe them: "Our AI-powered RTL engine...")


1. Language lock:
   - Write strictly in en.
   1.1 Perspective & Pronouns (MANDATORY):
      - Address the reader directly in SECOND PERSON in the target language.
      - Use natural second-person pronouns:
      Arabic: "أنت / لك / لديك / عملك / شركتك"
      English: "you / your"
      French: "vous / votre"
      German: "Sie / Ihr"
      Spanish: "tú/usted / tu/su"
      Turkish: "sen/siz / sizin"
      - At least one second-person construction in each paragraph.
      - Avoid detached third-person style like "businesses should..." unless used briefly for contrast.

2. Structure (CRITICAL):
   - **MAXIMUM 3 PARAGRAPHS PER SECTION.**
   - Base structure: 2-3 short paragraphs total. 
   - **STOP WRITING after the 3rd paragraph.** If you have more to say, use a list or table, OR simply stop. NEVER output a 4th paragraph.
   - PARAGRAPH WORD LIMIT (ABSOLUTE RULE): Each paragraph MUST NOT exceed 40 words.
     * Count your words. If a paragraph reaches 40 words → STOP and start a new paragraph immediately.
     * A line = ~14 words. So 40 words ≈ 3 short lines. This is the hard ceiling.
     * NEVER merge two paragraphs. NEVER write a 70-word paragraph split into two sentences.
   - DO NOT write wall-of-text paragraphs. Short = readable = better SEO.
   - If requires_list = true: add ONE markdown list (3–5 items) after paragraph 2.
   - If requires_table = true: add ONE markdown table (3+ rows) after paragraph 2.
   - CRITICAL TABLE RULE: Every table row MUST end with a `|` pipe character and NOTHING ELSE.
     - CORRECT: `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 |`
     - WRONG:   `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 | Some extra text here`
     - After the last `|` of any table row, the line MUST end immediately with no additional text.
     - A table MUST start on a NEW LINE and MUST be preceded by a blank line.
     - Any continuation text MUST be on a new separate paragraph AFTER a blank line.
   - FAQ sections keep H3 Q&A format only.

3. Keyword usage (Density target: 1.2%–1.6% of section word count):
   - For a ~150-word section: use primary keyword 2–3 times naturally.
   - First mention MUST be in the first paragraph.
   - At least one mention should be bolded (**keyword**) — but ONCE only per section.
   - Support with semantic synonyms and related phrases from "Required Keywords".
   - Never stuff: if keyword already has 2 mentions, switch to synonyms.
   - FAQ question headings should include keyword variations naturally (do NOT avoid the keyword entirely).

4. Local SEO:
   - If area exists, mention area once in first paragraph + one contextual local market sentence.
   - Do not repeat area excessively.

5. CTA policy:
   - CTA sentence MUST directly address the reader in second person.

   A. COMMERCIAL CTA DISTRIBUTION (For commercial/transactional articles — MANDATORY):
      Keep in mind: the goal is ALWAYS to sell the service. Never let the reader forget this.
      Inject CTAs at these specific positions in the article flow:

      * POSITION 1 — First Paragraph of Introduction: Include a direct, urgent CTA.
        Example: "Request a free consultation today and get your custom proposal within 24 hours."
      * POSITION 2 — After the 2nd Paragraph of Each Commercial H2 Section: One CTA sentence.
        Example: "Start building your platform with us now — spots fill fast."
      * POSITION 3 — Mid-Point of the Longest Section: One conversion-focused CTA.
        Example: "Book your strategy session this week and lead your market by summer."

        Example: "See how our services stack up — then get in touch to secure your slot."
      * POSITION 5 — The Section Immediately Before FAQ: Close with a strong CTA.
        Example: "Still have questions? Talk to our team now and get direct answers."
      * POSITION 6 — Article Conclusion (LAST LINE): High-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
        Example: "Don't let your competitors launch first — [Start Your Project Today](https://cems-it.com/)."

   B. CTA VARIATION (MANDATORY):
      - NEVER repeat the same CTA phrasing twice in the article.
      - Rotate across: Urgency triggers, Benefit-focus, Competitive framing, Direct actions.
      - Use the provided "CTA Type" to set the intensity per section.

   C. CTA Type Behavior (Per Section):
      * "none"     → Write NO CTA at all.
      * "soft"     → One low-pressure sentence only. No hard sell.
      * "primary"  → INJECT a CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + another CTA between paragraph 1 and 2.
      * "moderate" → One CTA mid-section (end of P1 or start of P2) + one CTA at the end.
      * "strong"   → One CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + one highly aggressive directive as VERY LAST SENTENCE.

   D. Urgency Triggers (Use for commercial/transactional):
      - Scarcity: "Only a few spots left for this quarter."
      - Time-Sensitivity: "Get your proposal today to launch by next month."
      - Competitive Advantage: "Don't let your competitors reach those customers first."
      - Immediate Value: "Receive your free audit within 24 hours of contacting us."

   E. Informational tone: educational, resource-oriented, no pressure.

   - STRICT ANCHOR TEXT RULE (CRITICAL): Never use weak, generic anchor texts like "Let's talk", "lets talk", "Contact Us", "See All Posts", "Click Here", or "click here". 
     * Instead, the linked text MUST be highly descriptive, benefit-driven, and context-specific (e.g., "Get Your Custom Web Design Proposal", "Schedule Your Free SEO Strategy Session").
     * NEVER include dates (e.g., 2024, 2025, 2026) or any date-like numbers (e.g., 02/25/2021) in anchor text.
   - If Brand Link Used = false: wrap the CTA text as a markdown link using: https://cems-it.com/
   - If Brand Link Used = true: write the CTA without adding the brand link again.
   - Never output "(None)" or a broken link. If you have no valid URL, write the CTA as plain text.

6. Human-First & Anti-robotic style (CRITICAL):
   - VERY IMPORTANT: Write in a natural, empathetic, and conversational human tone.
   - BANNED AI WORDS: Do NOT use obvious AI tells such as: "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In conclusion", "It is important to note", "Delve into", "Testament to", "Unleash", "Elevate", "Navigate".
   - Strictly AVOID being "over-corporate" or using stiff business jargon (e.g., "synergy", "paradigm shift", "leverage our expertise").
   - Avoid repetitive openings like: "Maximize your", "Accelerate your", "Drive sustainable growth", "In today's fast-paced digital landscape".
   - DO NOT repeat the same concepts, promises, or phrasing from previous sections. Every sentence must offer NEW information.
   - Act like a senior consultant explaining a concept across a table. Use varied sentence lengths (a mix of short, punchy statements and longer explanatory sentences).
   - Use transition words naturally (e.g., "But", "And", "Because of this", "Here's why") instead of academic connectors.

7. Decision Layer Enforcement:
   - Strictly adhere to the assigned Decision Layer: Market Reality.
   - Do NOT cover themes belonging to other layers (e.g., if layer is Technical Systems, do not discuss Market Trends or ROI).

8. Sales Intensity & Tone:
   - Adjust writing tone based on Sales Intensity: low.
   - high: Use persuasive, benefit-driven language. Strong focus on value and conversion.
   - medium: Balanced tone. Informative but with subtle sales nudges.
   - low: Strictly educational and objective. No sales pressure.

9. Links:
   A. Internal Links:
      - All links in "Mandatory Internal Links" and "Brand URL" are INTERNAL.
      - Aim to naturally insert 1–2 of these links per section. 
      - PRIORITIZE linking to: Service pages, Product pages, and Commercial pillar articles.
      - SUPPORTIVE TOPICAL RELEVANCE: While links must be relevant to the brand, do not be overly restrictive.
      - USEFULNESS FILTER: Only add a link if it is actually helpful to the reader's journey. Do NOT link to generic pages or irrelevant content just to meet a quota.
      - ANCHOR TEXT QUALITY (MANDATORY): 
         * Use highly descriptive, benefit-driven anchor text (e.g., [Explore Our Professional Web Development Services](https://...)).
         * NEVER use generic text (e.g., "Click here", "Read more", "lets talk") or include dates/numbers (e.g., 2024, 02/25/2021) in anchor text. 
         * ALWAYS focus on the specific service, product, or topic of the TARGET page.
      - CRITICAL: Skip any URL that already appears in "Used Internal Links".
   
   B. External Links:
      - Provide external links to credible sources (e.g., official organizations, well-known news outlets, or high-authority educational/research sites).
      - PROACTIVE NAMED ENTITY LINKING: When mentioning specific technologies (e.g., [React](https://react.dev), [PHP](https://www.php.net)), frameworks, official government initiatives (e.g., [Saudi Vision 2030](https://www.vision2030.gov.sa)), or recognized organizations, ALWAYS provide a link to their official homepage or documentation.
      - NEVER link to competitors who are targeting the same primary keyword: "Web Design Agency".
      - External links MUST add significant value and support the reader's understanding of the topic.
      - QUALITY OVER QUANTITY: Do not add a link unless it is truly relevant. Broken links or links to low-quality/spam sites will cause the section to be REJECTED.
      - Format as natural inline markdown: [Catchy Descriptive Title](https://actual-url.com)
10. FAQ behavior:
   - If section_type = faq, you MUST answer EVERY question listed in the "Questions" array.
   - Use an H3 heading for each question, followed by a concise, unique, and helpful answer. Exactly 4-6 questions MUST be present.
   - Commercial FAQ = objection handling.
   - Informational FAQ = educational clarity, no selling.
   - Apply FAQ Schema (HTML Microdata) to the questions and answers when possible.

11. Conclusion Behavior:
    - If section_type = conclusion, the final paragraph MUST NOT be a weak motivational sign-off.
    - Instead, it MUST culminate in a high-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
    - Follow the strict anchor text rule (highly descriptive, benefit-driven).

12. Case Study Behavior:
    - If section_type = case_study, you MUST highlight the project/portfolio link assigned to this section (if any).
    - Describe the real-world impact, process, and results in a confident, story-driven way.
    - Do NOT make up fake metrics; focus on the proven capability to deliver high-end solutions.

- Markdown only
- Do not repeat the section heading
- No explanations, no metadata

STRICT WRITING RULES

1. Language lock:
   - Write strictly in en.
   1.1 Perspective & Pronouns (MANDATORY):
      - Address the reader directly in SECOND PERSON in the target language.
      - Use natural second-person pronouns:
      Arabic: "أنت / لك / لديك / عملك / شركتك"
      English: "you / your"
      French: "vous / votre"
      German: "Sie / Ihr"
      Spanish: "tú/usted / tu/su"
      Turkish: "sen/siz / sizin"
      - At least one second-person construction in each paragraph.
      - Avoid detached third-person style like "businesses should..." unless used briefly for contrast.

2. Structure (CRITICAL):
   - **MAXIMUM 3 PARAGRAPHS PER SECTION.**
   - Base structure: 2-3 short paragraphs total. 
   - **STOP WRITING after the 3rd paragraph.** If you have more to say, use a list or table, OR simply stop. NEVER output a 4th paragraph.
   - PARAGRAPH WORD LIMIT (ABSOLUTE RULE): Each paragraph MUST NOT exceed 40 words.
     * Count your words. If a paragraph reaches 40 words → STOP and start a new paragraph immediately.
     * A line = ~14 words. So 40 words ≈ 3 short lines. This is the hard ceiling.
     * NEVER merge two paragraphs. NEVER write a 70-word paragraph split into two sentences.
   - DO NOT write wall-of-text paragraphs. Short = readable = better SEO.
   - If requires_list = true: add ONE markdown list (3–5 items) after paragraph 2.
   - If requires_table = true: add ONE markdown table (3+ rows) after paragraph 2.
   - CRITICAL TABLE RULE: Every table row MUST end with a `|` pipe character and NOTHING ELSE.
     - CORRECT: `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 |`
     - WRONG:   `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 | Some extra text here`
     - After the last `|` of any table row, the line MUST end immediately with no additional text.
     - A table MUST start on a NEW LINE and MUST be preceded by a blank line.
     - Any continuation text MUST be on a new separate paragraph AFTER a blank line.
   - FAQ sections keep H3 Q&A format only.

3. Keyword usage (Density target: 1.2%–1.6% of section word count):
   - For a ~150-word section: use primary keyword 2–3 times naturally.
   - First mention MUST be in the first paragraph.
   - At least one mention should be bolded (**keyword**) — but ONCE only per section.
   - Support with semantic synonyms and related phrases from "Required Keywords".
   - Never stuff: if keyword already has 2 mentions, switch to synonyms.
   - FAQ question headings should include keyword variations naturally (do NOT avoid the keyword entirely).

STRICT WRITING RULES
0. Source-Grounded Writing (CRITICAL - MANDATORY):
   - Real text from the brand's website is provided above (Brand Source Text).
   - YOU MUST PRIORITIZE THIS TEXT over your general knowledge.
   - Extract specific facts, methodologies, or features RELEVANT to this section from that text.
   - DO NOT HALLUCINATE. If the brand does not mention a specific service or feature in the source text, DO NOT claim they offer it.
   - Every major claim MUST be grounded in the Brand Source Text. If the text says they use "PHP", do not write that they use "React" just because it's popular.
   - For 'Why Choose Us' or 'Case Study' sections: your reasons MUST come from the Source Text.

   0.1. Brand Specificity & Attribution (HIGH PRIORITY):
      - EVERY technical claim or methodology MUST be explicitly attributed to the brand name (e.g., "At CEMS IT, our process involves...", "CEMS IT utilizes...").
      - MINIMUM 2 SPECIFIC FACTS: You MUST mention at least TWO distinct technical or process-related facts extracted from the Brand Source Text (e.g., specific frameworks, sprint types, localized features).
      - ANTI-BANAL POLICY: Strictly PROHIBITED from using generic "marketing fluff" like:
        * "We use the latest technologies" (Instead name them: "We use Node.js and React")
        * "We prioritize quality/clients" (Instead explain HOW: "We use 3-stage QA testing")
        * "We provide innovative solutions" (Instead describe them: "Our AI-powered RTL engine...")


1. Language lock:
   - Write strictly in en.
   1.1 Perspective & Pronouns (MANDATORY):
      - Address the reader directly in SECOND PERSON in the target language.
      - Use natural second-person pronouns:
      Arabic: "أنت / لك / لديك / عملك / شركتك"
      English: "you / your"
      French: "vous / votre"
      German: "Sie / Ihr"
      Spanish: "tú/usted / tu/su"
      Turkish: "sen/siz / sizin"
      - At least one second-person construction in each paragraph.
      - Avoid detached third-person style like "businesses should..." unless used briefly for contrast.

2. Structure (CRITICAL):
   - **MAXIMUM 3 PARAGRAPHS PER SECTION.**
   - Base structure: 2-3 short paragraphs total. 
   - **STOP WRITING after the 3rd paragraph.** If you have more to say, use a list or table, OR simply stop. NEVER output a 4th paragraph.
   - PARAGRAPH WORD LIMIT (ABSOLUTE RULE): Each paragraph MUST NOT exceed 40 words.
     * Count your words. If a paragraph reaches 40 words → STOP and start a new paragraph immediately.
     * A line = ~14 words. So 40 words ≈ 3 short lines. This is the hard ceiling.
     * NEVER merge two paragraphs. NEVER write a 70-word paragraph split into two sentences.
   - DO NOT write wall-of-text paragraphs. Short = readable = better SEO.
   - If requires_list = true: add ONE markdown list (3–5 items) after paragraph 2.
   - If requires_table = true: add ONE markdown table (3+ rows) after paragraph 2.
   - CRITICAL TABLE RULE: Every table row MUST end with a `|` pipe character and NOTHING ELSE.
     - CORRECT: `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 |`
     - WRONG:   `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 | Some extra text here`
     - After the last `|` of any table row, the line MUST end immediately with no additional text.
     - A table MUST start on a NEW LINE and MUST be preceded by a blank line.
     - Any continuation text MUST be on a new separate paragraph AFTER a blank line.
   - FAQ sections keep H3 Q&A format only.

3. Keyword usage (Density target: 1.2%–1.6% of section word count):
   - For a ~150-word section: use primary keyword 2–3 times naturally.
   - First mention MUST be in the first paragraph.
   - At least one mention should be bolded (**keyword**) — but ONCE only per section.
   - Support with semantic synonyms and related phrases from "Required Keywords".
   - Never stuff: if keyword already has 2 mentions, switch to synonyms.
   - FAQ question headings should include keyword variations naturally (do NOT avoid the keyword entirely).

4. Local SEO:
   - If area exists, mention area once in first paragraph + one contextual local market sentence.
   - Do not repeat area excessively.

5. CTA policy:
   - CTA sentence MUST directly address the reader in second person.

   A. COMMERCIAL CTA DISTRIBUTION (For commercial/transactional articles — MANDATORY):
      Keep in mind: the goal is ALWAYS to sell the service. Never let the reader forget this.
      Inject CTAs at these specific positions in the article flow:

      * POSITION 1 — First Paragraph of Introduction: Include a direct, urgent CTA.
        Example: "Request a free consultation today and get your custom proposal within 24 hours."
      * POSITION 2 — After the 2nd Paragraph of Each Commercial H2 Section: One CTA sentence.
        Example: "Start building your platform with us now — spots fill fast."
      * POSITION 3 — Mid-Point of the Longest Section: One conversion-focused CTA.
        Example: "Book your strategy session this week and lead your market by summer."
      * POSITION 4 — Just Before Any Table (if requires_table=true): A CTA hook.
        Example: "See how our services stack up — then get in touch to secure your slot."
      * POSITION 5 — The Section Immediately Before FAQ: Close with a strong CTA.
        Example: "Still have questions? Talk to our team now and get direct answers."
      * POSITION 6 — Article Conclusion (LAST LINE): High-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
        Example: "Don't let your competitors launch first — [Start Your Project Today](https://cems-it.com/)."

   B. CTA VARIATION (MANDATORY):
      - NEVER repeat the same CTA phrasing twice in the article.
      - Rotate across: Urgency triggers, Benefit-focus, Competitive framing, Direct actions.
      - Use the provided "CTA Type" to set the intensity per section.

   C. CTA Type Behavior (Per Section):
      * "none"     → Write NO CTA at all.
      * "soft"     → One low-pressure sentence only. No hard sell.
      * "primary"  → INJECT a CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + another CTA between paragraph 1 and 2.
      * "moderate" → One CTA mid-section (end of P1 or start of P2) + one CTA at the end.
      * "strong"   → One CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + one highly aggressive directive as VERY LAST SENTENCE.

   D. Urgency Triggers (Use for commercial/transactional):
      - Scarcity: "Only a few spots left for this quarter."
      - Time-Sensitivity: "Get your proposal today to launch by next month."
      - Competitive Advantage: "Don't let your competitors reach those customers first."
      - Immediate Value: "Receive your free audit within 24 hours of contacting us."

   E. Informational tone: educational, resource-oriented, no pressure.

   - STRICT ANCHOR TEXT RULE (CRITICAL): Never use weak, generic anchor texts like "Let's talk", "lets talk", "Contact Us", "See All Posts", "Click Here", or "click here". 
     * Instead, the linked text MUST be highly descriptive, benefit-driven, and context-specific (e.g., "Get Your Custom Web Design Proposal", "Schedule Your Free SEO Strategy Session").
     * NEVER include dates (e.g., 2024, 2025, 2026) or any date-like numbers (e.g., 02/25/2021) in anchor text.
   - If Brand Link Used = false: wrap the CTA text as a markdown link using: https://cems-it.com/
   - If Brand Link Used = true: write the CTA without adding the brand link again.
   - Never output "(None)" or a broken link. If you have no valid URL, write the CTA as plain text.

6. Human-First & Anti-robotic style (CRITICAL):
   - VERY IMPORTANT: Write in a natural, empathetic, and conversational human tone.
   - BANNED AI WORDS: Do NOT use obvious AI tells such as: "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In conclusion", "It is important to note", "Delve into", "Testament to", "Unleash", "Elevate", "Navigate".
   - Strictly AVOID being "over-corporate" or using stiff business jargon (e.g., "synergy", "paradigm shift", "leverage our expertise").
   - Avoid repetitive openings like: "Maximize your", "Accelerate your", "Drive sustainable growth", "In today's fast-paced digital landscape".
   - DO NOT repeat the same concepts, promises, or phrasing from previous sections. Every sentence must offer NEW information.
   - Act like a senior consultant explaining a concept across a table. Use varied sentence lengths (a mix of short, punchy statements and longer explanatory sentences).
   - Use transition words naturally (e.g., "But", "And", "Because of this", "Here's why") instead of academic connectors.

7. Decision Layer Enforcement:
   - Strictly adhere to the assigned Decision Layer: Implementation Process.
   - Do NOT cover themes belonging to other layers (e.g., if layer is Technical Systems, do not discuss Market Trends or ROI).

8. Sales Intensity & Tone:
   - Adjust writing tone based on Sales Intensity: high.
   - high: Use persuasive, benefit-driven language. Strong focus on value and conversion.
   - medium: Balanced tone. Informative but with subtle sales nudges.
   - low: Strictly educational and objective. No sales pressure.

9. Links:
   A. Internal Links:
      - All links in "Mandatory Internal Links" and "Brand URL" are INTERNAL.
      - Aim to naturally insert 1–2 of these links per section. 
      - PRIORITIZE linking to: Service pages, Product pages, and Commercial pillar articles.

         * ALWAYS focus on the specific service, product, or topic of the TARGET page.
      - CRITICAL: Skip any URL that already appears in "Used Internal Links".
   
   B. External Links:
      - Provide external links to credible sources (e.g., official organizations, well-known news outlets, or high-authority educational/research sites).
      - PROACTIVE NAMED ENTITY LINKING: When mentioning specific technologies (e.g., [React](https://react.dev), [PHP](https://www.php.net)), frameworks, official government initiatives (e.g., [Saudi Vision 2030](https://www.vision2030.gov.sa)), or recognized organizations, ALWAYS provide a link to their official homepage or documentation.

## Common Questions About Choosing a Web Design Agency in Riyadh


<!-- section_id: sec_07 -->

### How do I ensure my website complies with Saudi hosting and data regulations?
To operate legally in Riyadh, your site must align with the **National Data Management Office (NDMO)** standards. Choosing a **Web Design Agency** that understands local hosting requirements ensures your data remains secure and compliant with KSA laws.

### Which payment gateways are essential for a Riyadh-based e-commerce site?
Your customers expect seamless transactions through local providers like **STC Pay**, **Mada**, and **Moyasar**. Integrating these specific gateways reduces checkout friction and builds trust within the local market, ensuring your Websites function effectively for Saudi users.

### Does my website need to be ZATCA compliant for e-invoicing?
Yes, if you conduct B2B or B2C transactions, your platform must integrate with ZATCA Phase 2 requirements. A specialized **Web Design Riyadh** partner will implement the necessary technical architecture to automate tax-compliant invoicing directly through your backend system.

### How does right-to-left (RTL) design impact my user experience?
Arabic-first navigation is not just about translation; it requires a complete structural flip of the UI. Professional **Design Services** ensure that menus, buttons, and layouts feel natural to Saudi users, preventing the common alignment errors found in generic templates.

### Why is local hosting better for my Riyadh business?
Local servers significantly reduce latency for your primary audience, leading to faster load speeds. Because Saudi consumers favor mobile browsing, a **Web Design Agency** prioritizing localized infrastructure will help you achieve the high performance required for modern Design Services.

### What are the specific UI/UX preferences for the Saudi market?
Riyadh users often prefer clean, high-contrast layouts with culturally resonant imagery and prominent contact options like WhatsApp. Your digital presence should balance global aesthetic standards with the specific browsing habits and visual expectations of the local KSA community.

Role:
You are an SEO Content Architect.
Generate OUTLINE ONLY.

Hard Rules:
1. Language: all headings in en only.

2. Intent Distribution (MANDATORY):
   - brand_commercial (Sales):
     * Ratio: 70% Commercial/Transactional sections, 30% Informational.
     * Must include: "Why Choose Us" (Uniqueness), "Proof/Trust", and "Implementation/Process".
     * Focus: Converting segments (e.g., service for startups, service for high-growth firms).
   - informational (Guide):
     * Ratio: 100% Educational/Informational.
     * NO CTAs allowed in body sections.
     * Focus: Topical authority and user value.

3. FAQ Section Rules:
   - Exactly ONE FAQ section (H2) with 4 to 6 FAQs related to the primary keyword.
   - Each FAQ must be unique, helpful, and include keyword variations.
   - At least one FAQ question MUST address a local market need/context.

4. No duplicate H2 meanings.

5. Domain Separation: 
   - Each H2 must focus on a distinct "Decision Layer" to prevent semantic overlap.:
     - Market Reality: Landscape/Trends.
     - Business Risk: Pain points/Failure of generic solutions.
     - Technical Systems: "How it works" / Proprietary mechanisms.
     - Proof Layer: Evidence, ROI, history.
     - Implementation: Steps/Next actions.

6. Decision Layers (Assign one per section):
   - Market Reality: External trends/landscape.
   - Business Risk: Why generic fails (Urgency).
   - Technical Systems: Unique mechanisms/frameworks (The "How").
   - Proof Layer: ROI, metrics, trust.
   - Implementation Process: Operational path/next steps.

7. If area exists:
   - First H2 must include area naturally.
   - Each section must have a different localized_angle.
   7b. Primary Keyword in Headings:
      - The primary keyword (or a close semantic variant) MUST appear in at least 2 heading_text values.
      - It MUST appear in the introduction heading_text.
      - It MUST appear in at least one other H2 (e.g., Proof Layer or Technical Systems).

8. Human-First & Anti-Robotic Style:
   - Headings (heading_text) MUST sound natural, conversational, and human. 
   - STRICTLY AVOID over-corporate jargon (e.g., "Synergistic Marketing Solutions", "Unlocking Paradigm Shifts").
   - DO NOT create repetitive heading structures (e.g., avoid starting multiple headings with "The Importance of...", "Benefits of...").
   - AVOID typical AI heading patterns like "Navigating the complexities of...".
   - In remaining sections use semantic variations naturally in the heading text.

9. CTA Policy (distribute across article based on intent):
   For brand_commercial:
      - ALWAYS REMEMBER: The goal is to SELL. Every section is an opportunity to convert the reader.
      - section_type = introduction: cta_type = "primary", cta_position = "first_paragraph".
        CTA must be a direct call (e.g. "request a consultation", "start your project") + brand URL.
      - section_type = core or benefits: cta_type = "primary", cta_position = "middle".
        CTA must push the reader to one clear next action.
      - section_type = proof: cta_type = "moderate", cta_position = "last_sentence".
      - section_type = process: cta_type = "moderate", cta_position = "last_sentence".
      - section_type = why_choose_us: cta_type = "primary", cta_position = "middle".
      - section_type = case_study: cta_type = "moderate", cta_position = "last_sentence".
      - section_type = conclusion: cta_type = "strong", cta_position = "last_sentence".
        CTA must be high-urgency with brand URL link.
      - section_type = faq: cta_type = "none".
      - The section IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the FAQ section: cta_type = "strong", cta_position = "last_sentence".
        This is a mandatory conversion checkpoint — close with maximum urgency before the reader enters FAQ.
      - All other sections: cta_type = "moderate" minimum.
      - CRITICAL — CTA VARIATION: Each section's CTA MUST use DIFFERENT phrasing and framing.
        Rotate through: Scarcity, Time-sensitivity, Benefit-focus, Competitive advantage, Direct action.
        NEVER repeat the same CTA sentence twice in the article.
   For informational:
      - NO CTAs in body. Only soft resource links allowed.
      - section_type = introduction: cta_type = "soft", cta_position = "last_sentence".
        Soft CTA = "Learn more", "Explore the full guide", or resource link. No hard sell.
      - section_type = conclusion: cta_type = "soft", cta_position = "last_sentence".
      - All other sections: cta_type = "none".

10. Sales Intensity (Distribute across sections):
   - high: strong persuasive tone, clear value proposition (e.g., Intro, Proof, Conclusion).
   - medium: balanced education and selling (e.g., Technical Systems).
   - low: purely educational, no selling (e.g., FAQ, Market Reality).

11. Rich-format requirement:
   - At least ONE non-FAQ section must have requires_table = true.
   - At least TWO non-FAQ sections must have requires_list = true.
   - Exactly ONE table (comparison or process) in a core section.
   - At least TWO bulleted/numbered lists.

12. Conclusion enforcement (CRITICAL):
    - The VERY LAST section of the outline MUST have `section_type: "conclusion"`.
    - Do NOT name it "core" or anything else. It must be explicitly tagged as "conclusion".
    - It must have `cta_type: "strong"` and `cta_position: "last_sentence"`.

13. Table placement:
   - Prefer table in "Proof Layer" or "Implementation Process" only.

14. Mandatory section types enforcement:
   - You MUST include every section_type listed in Mandatory Section Types exactly as-is.
   - Do not rename, paraphrase, or merge them.
   14b. Superlative Justification (Proof Layer):
      - If Article Title uses "Top", "Best", "Leading", or "#1", the "Proof Layer" section MUST include a `content_goal` to justify this claim.
      - Justification must focus on: Years of experience, Number of successful projects, Unique proprietary methodology, or measurable ROI results.
   14c. Mandatory Section Separation:
      - "Why Choose Us" (why_choose_us) and "Proof" (proof) are separate entities.
      - why_choose_us focuses on the USER'S benefits and unique value proposition.
      - proof focuses on OBJECTIVE evidence (metrics, history, data).
      - NEVER merge these into a single section.
   14d. Case Study / Portfolio:
      - If "case_study" is dynamically required, find the most relevant URL in the "Internal Links" list (Project, Portfolio, or Success Story) and dedicate this section to it.
      - Make the heading sound natural (e.g., "See How We Helped [Client/Industry]").
   14e. Why Choose Us:
      - Ensure the heading text for the 'why_choose_us' section uses a strong converting phrase like "Why Choose [Brand Name] for [Keyword]" or similar.

15. Hierarchical Depth (Featured Snippets):
   - At least 2 non-FAQ H2 sections MUST have 1-2 internal H3 subheadings.
   - Use H3 to break down: "Comparison of services", "Technical components", "Step-by-step processes", or "Specific customer categories".
   - H3 headings must directly support the parent H2 and follow it immediately in the JSON list.
   - This prevents flat content and maximizes Featured Snippet potential.

16. Strategic Linking (Internal):
   - You are provided with a list of "Internal Links" (URLs).
   - LINK QUOTA (STRICT): Assign between 3 and 6 total unique internal links for the entire article.
   - **MAXIMUM ONE (1) INTERNAL LINK PER SECTION.**
   - ANCHOR DIVERSITY: Each assigned link MUST have a unique and highly descriptive anchor text (`text` field) that identifies the target page's content (e.g., "[custom React development solutions](url)").
   - PRIORITIZATION: Prioritize linking to Service or Product pages. Avoid generic "About" or "Contact" pages unless they are the primary goal.
   - DISTRIBUTION: Distribute these links across different sections (max 1 link per section) by adding them to the `assigned_links` array. No section should have more than 1 internal link.

17. Reader Addressing:
   - content_goal and content_angle must be reader-directed (second-person oriented), not generic third-person.

STRICT WRITING RULES
0. Source-Grounded Writing (CRITICAL - MANDATORY):
   - Real text from the brand's website is provided above (Brand Source Text).
   - YOU MUST PRIORITIZE THIS TEXT over your general knowledge.
   - Extract specific facts, methodologies, or features RELEVANT to this section from that text.
   - DO NOT HALLUCINATE. If the brand does not mention a specific service or feature in the source text, DO NOT claim they offer it.
   - Every major claim MUST be grounded in the Brand Source Text. If the text says they use "PHP", do not write that they use "React" just because it's popular.
   - For 'Why Choose Us' or 'Case Study' sections: your reasons MUST come from the Source Text.

   0.1. Brand Specificity & Attribution (HIGH PRIORITY):
      - EVERY technical claim or methodology MUST be explicitly attributed to the brand name (e.g., "At CEMS IT, our process involves...", "CEMS IT utilizes...").
      - MINIMUM 2 SPECIFIC FACTS: You MUST mention at least TWO distinct technical or process-related facts extracted from the Brand Source Text (e.g., specific frameworks, sprint types, localized features).
      - ANTI-BANAL POLICY: Strictly PROHIBITED from using generic "marketing fluff" like:
        * "We use the latest technologies" (Instead name them: "We use Node.js and React")
        * "We prioritize quality/clients" (Instead explain HOW: "We use 3-stage QA testing")
        * "We provide innovative solutions" (Instead describe them: "Our AI-powered RTL engine...")


1. Language lock:
   - Write strictly in en.
   1.1 Perspective & Pronouns (MANDATORY):
      - Address the reader directly in SECOND PERSON in the target language.
      - Use natural second-person pronouns:
      Arabic: "أنت / لك / لديك / عملك / شركتك"
      English: "you / your"
      French: "vous / votre"
      German: "Sie / Ihr"
      Spanish: "tú/usted / tu/su"
      Turkish: "sen/siz / sizin"
      - At least one second-person construction in each paragraph.
      - Avoid detached third-person style like "businesses should..." unless used briefly for contrast.

2. Structure (CRITICAL):
   - **MAXIMUM 3 PARAGRAPHS PER SECTION.**
   - Base structure: 2-3 short paragraphs total. 
   - **STOP WRITING after the 3rd paragraph.** If you have more to say, use a list or table, OR simply stop. NEVER output a 4th paragraph.
   - PARAGRAPH WORD LIMIT (ABSOLUTE RULE): Each paragraph MUST NOT exceed 40 words.
     * Count your words. If a paragraph reaches 40 words → STOP and start a new paragraph immediately.
     * A line = ~14 words. So 40 words ≈ 3 short lines. This is the hard ceiling.
     * NEVER merge two paragraphs. NEVER write a 70-word paragraph split into two sentences.
   - DO NOT write wall-of-text paragraphs. Short = readable = better SEO.
   - If requires_list = true: add ONE markdown list (3–5 items) after paragraph 2.
   - If requires_table = true: add ONE markdown table (3+ rows) after paragraph 2.
   - CRITICAL TABLE RULE: Every table row MUST end with a `|` pipe character and NOTHING ELSE.
     - CORRECT: `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 |`
     - WRONG:   `| Cell 1 | Cell 2 | Cell 3 | Some extra text here`
     - After the last `|` of any table row, the line MUST end immediately with no additional text.
     - A table MUST start on a NEW LINE and MUST be preceded by a blank line.
     - Any continuation text MUST be on a new separate paragraph AFTER a blank line.
   - FAQ sections keep H3 Q&A format only.

3. Keyword usage (Density target: 1.2%–1.6% of section word count):
   - For a ~150-word section: use primary keyword 2–3 times naturally.
   - First mention MUST be in the first paragraph.
   - At least one mention should be bolded (**keyword**) — but ONCE only per section.
   - Support with semantic synonyms and related phrases from "Required Keywords".
   - Never stuff: if keyword already has 2 mentions, switch to synonyms.
   - FAQ question headings should include keyword variations naturally (do NOT avoid the keyword entirely).

4. Local SEO:
   - If area exists, mention area once in first paragraph + one contextual local market sentence.
   - Do not repeat area excessively.

5. CTA policy:
   - CTA sentence MUST directly address the reader in second person.

   A. COMMERCIAL CTA DISTRIBUTION (For commercial/transactional articles — MANDATORY):
      Keep in mind: the goal is ALWAYS to sell the service. Never let the reader forget this.
      Inject CTAs at these specific positions in the article flow:

      * POSITION 1 — First Paragraph of Introduction: Include a direct, urgent CTA.
        Example: "Request a free consultation today and get your custom proposal within 24 hours."
      * POSITION 2 — After the 2nd Paragraph of Each Commercial H2 Section: One CTA sentence.
        Example: "Start building your platform with us now — spots fill fast."
      * POSITION 3 — Mid-Point of the Longest Section: One conversion-focused CTA.
        Example: "Book your strategy session this week and lead your market by summer."
      * POSITION 4 — Just Before Any Table (if requires_table=true): A CTA hook.
        Example: "See how our services stack up — then get in touch to secure your slot."
      * POSITION 5 — The Section Immediately Before FAQ: Close with a strong CTA.
        Example: "Still have questions? Talk to our team now and get direct answers."
      * POSITION 6 — Article Conclusion (LAST LINE): High-urgency, conversion-focused directive.
        Example: "Don't let your competitors launch first — [Start Your Project Today](https://cems-it.com/)."

   B. CTA VARIATION (MANDATORY):
      - NEVER repeat the same CTA phrasing twice in the article.
      - Rotate across: Urgency triggers, Benefit-focus, Competitive framing, Direct actions.
      - Use the provided "CTA Type" to set the intensity per section.

   C. CTA Type Behavior (Per Section):
      * "none"     → Write NO CTA at all.
      * "soft"     → One low-pressure sentence only. No hard sell.
      * "primary"  → INJECT a CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + another CTA between paragraph 1 and 2.
      * "moderate" → One CTA mid-section (end of P1 or start of P2) + one CTA at the end.
      * "strong"   → One CTA in FIRST PARAGRAPH + one highly aggressive directive as VERY LAST SENTENCE.

   D. Urgency Triggers (Use for commercial/transactional):
      - Scarcity: "Only a few spots left for this quarter."
      - Time-Sensitivity: "Get your proposal today to launch by next month."
      - Competitive Advantage: "Don't let your competitors reach those customers first."
      - Immediate Value: "Receive your free audit within 24 hours of contacting us."

   E. Informational tone: educational, resource-oriented, no pressure.

    - LINKING POLICY (CRITICAL):
      - **MAXIMUM ONE (1) INTERNAL LINK PER SECTION.** 
      - If multiple links are provided in "Mandatory Internal Links", CHOOSE ONLY THE MOST RELEVANT ONE.
      - **CONTEXTUAL RELEVANCE:** The link MUST be naturally woven into a sentence where it has a strong logical connection to the surrounding text. Do not just drop a link at the end.
      - **STRICT ANCHOR TEXT RULE (CRITICAL):**
        * The anchor text MUST be highly descriptive, benefit-driven, and context-specific.
        * GOOD: "[our comprehensive UI/UX design process](url)", "[custom React-based web development](url)"
        * BAD: "[click here](url)", "[our services](url)", "[projects](url)", "[Riyadh](url)", "Let's talk", "Contact Us".
      - NEVER include dates (e.g., 2024, 2025, 2026) or any date-like numbers in anchor text.
      - DEDUPLICATION: Do not use the same URL or the same anchor text twice in the entire article.

6. Human-First & Anti-robotic style (CRITICAL):
   - VERY IMPORTANT: Write in a natural, empathetic, and conversational human tone.
   - BANNED AI WORDS: Do NOT use obvious AI tells such as: "Moreover", "Furthermore", "In conclusion", "It is important to note", "Delve into", "Testament to", "Unleash", "Elevate", "Navigate".
   - Strictly AVOID being "over-corporate" or using stiff business jargon (e.g., "synergy", "paradigm shift", "leverage our expertise").
   - Avoid repetitive openings like: "Maximize your", "Accelerate your", "Drive sustainable growth", "In today's fast-paced digital landscape".
   - DO NOT repeat the same concepts, promises, or phrasing from previous sections. Every sentence must offer NEW information.
   - Act like a senior consultant explaining a concept across a table. Use varied sentence lengths (a mix of short, punchy statements and longer explanatory sentences).
   - Use transition words naturally (e.g., "But", "And", "Because of this", "Here's why") instead of academic connectors.

7. Decision Layer Enforcement:
   - Strictly adhere to the assigned Decision Layer: Market Reality.
   - Do NOT cover themes belonging to other layers (e.g., if layer is Technical Systems, do not discuss Market Trends or ROI).

8. Sales Intensity & Tone:
   - Adjust writing tone based on Sales Intensity: high.
   - high: Use persuasive, benefit-driven language. Strong focus on value and conversion.
   - medium: Balanced tone. Informative but with subtle sales nudges.
   - low: Strictly educational and objective. No sales pressure.

9. Links:
   A. Internal Links:
      - All links in "Mandatory Internal Links" and "Brand URL" are INTERNAL.

Their approach is characterized by a focus on UI/UX design that prioritizes usability and intuitive interfaces, integrating custom branding with technical development under one roof to ensure high-quality, on-time delivery. Furthermore, they differentiate their service by offering "smart AI tools" and bots to automate user experiences within their web platforms. Their target clients span diverse sectors including education (Rage3), real estate, legal consultation, construction (Builders for Constructions and Real Estate), and even government entities like the Qatar General Authority of Customs."
